name: adapter_registry_v1
description: Adapter registry training data covering registration, version management, lineage tracking, status lifecycle, and registry operations. Encodes the full adapter_id naming convention ({tenant}/{domain}/{purpose}/{revision}), BLAKE3 bundle hashing, release state machine (draft -> training -> ready -> active -> deprecated -> retired), trust propagation from datasets, rollback mechanics, and cross-tenant federation queries.

entries.notes: 2000 examples: 400 registration, 400 version management, 400 lineage queries, 400 status lifecycle, 400 registry operations. Shuffled.

domain.lineage_chain: source_document -> dataset_version -> training_run -> adapter_version -> deployment -> routing

provenance.review_notes: Hand-authored generator with deterministic seed (42). Covers all registration validation, version lifecycle, lineage queries, state transitions, and operational patterns grounded in the actual adapteros-db adapter_repositories module.

evaluation_gates: All positive registration examples use valid {tenant}/{domain}/{purpose}/{revision} naming
All negative registration examples correctly identify the naming or uniqueness violation
Release state transitions conform to the valid_transitions map
All negative lifecycle examples identify the illegal transition and the valid alternative
Lineage queries return consistent provenance chains with matching IDs and hashes
Trust propagation examples correctly apply the blocked > warn > unknown > allowed hierarchy
Rollback examples verify target is in Ready or Deprecated state before promoting
Archive examples enforce version freeze after archival
Bundle hashes are 64-character hex strings consistent with BLAKE3 output
Revision gap rule (max 5) is enforced in all version creation examples
